---
title: "Suits Viewership Report"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

# *Suits* — Viewership Report

## 1. Description

*Suits* is an American legal drama television series created by Aaron Korsh. The show premiered on June 23, 2011, on USA Network. It follows Mike Ross, a brilliant college dropout, who begins working as a law associate for Harvey Specter despite never attending law school. The series focuses on corporate law cases and relationships within the firm.

---

## 2. Show Image

![Suits Title Card](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/7/7f/Suits_title_card.png)

---

## 3. Basic Statistics

- Total seasons: 9  
- Total episodes: 134  
- Premiere viewership: ~4.64 million  
- Final season viewership: ~1.1 million  
- Overall trend: gradual decline in audience over time  

---

## 4. Load Libraries

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```

---

## 5. Viewership Data

```{r}
seasons <- 1:9
viewers <- c(4.2, 3.5, 3.0, 2.5, 2.2, 1.8, 1.5, 1.3, 1.1)

df <- data.frame(seasons, viewers)
```

---

## 6. Viewership Over Time

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = seasons, y = viewers)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Average Viewership of Suits by Season",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Viewers (millions)"
  )
```

---

## 7. Season-to-Season Changes

```{r}
changes <- diff(viewers)

df_changes <- data.frame(
  seasons = seasons[-1],
  changes = changes
)
```

```{r}
ggplot(df_changes, aes(x = seasons, y = changes)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Change in Viewership Between Seasons",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Change in Viewers (millions)"
  )
```

---

## 8. Observed Trends

The viewership of *Suits* shows a steady decline over time.

- From Season 1 (4.2 million) to Season 3 (3.0 million), viewership decreased by **1.2 million**.
- Between Season 3 and Season 5, it dropped further by **0.8 million**.
- From Season 6 (1.8 million) to Season 9 (1.1 million), the decline was **0.7 million**.

Overall, the show lost approximately **3.1 million viewers** from its early seasons to its conclusion. Despite this decline, it maintained a consistent audience throughout its run.

---
